fullText:
Republic of the Philippines
HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES
Quezon City, Metro Manila
TWENTIETH (20th) CONGRESS
First Regular Session
House Resolution No. 21
___________________________________________________________________
Introduced by
Rep. ROBERT NAZAL
___________________________________________________________________
A RESOLUTION
DIRECTING THE APPROPRIATE COMMITTEE TO REVIEW PRESIDENTIAL
DECREE NO. 114, ALSO KNOWN AS THE PAWNSHOP REGULATION ACT,
AND TO CONDUCT AN INQUIRY, IN AID OF LEGISLATION, ON ITS
IMPLEMENTATION TO ADDRESS LAX ENFORCEMENT THAT ENABLES
VIOLATIONS OF THE ANTI-MONEY LAUNDERING ACT, THE ANTI-FENCING
LAW, AND OTHER RELATED OFFENSES
WHEREAS, Presidential Decree No. 114, or the Pawnshop Regulation Act, was
enacted on January 29, 1973, to establish a comprehensive regulatory framework
governing pawnshop operation and to ensure the protection of pawnshop clients;
WHEREAS, the pawnshop industry serves as a critical source of financial services in
the Philippines, particularly among underserved communities, by providing secured
loans against valuable personal property;
WHEREAS, there have been alarming reports indicating weak enforcement of PD
No. 114, thereby creating opportunities for violations of the Anti-Money Laundering
Act, the Anti-Fencing Law, and other related laws;
WHEREAS, recent incidents have revealed the acceptance of stolen watches and
other valuable items by certain pawnshops without adequate verification of
ownership, raising serious concerns about complicity in money laundering and
fencing;
WHEREAS, in some cases, stolen items initially rejected were subsequently sold to
third parties following internal interventions, in apparent contravention of the
Anti-Fencing Law and related regulations;
WHEREAS, the subsequent refusal of some pawnshop representatives to cooperate
with the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (CIDG), citing data-privacy
concerns, has obstructed legitimate law-enforcement investigations and impeded
access to evidence necessary for the prosecution of criminal activities;
July 3, 2025
11:28 am

-- 1 of 2 --

WHEREAS, these events underscore the urgent need to review PD No. 114, close
regulatory loopholes, reinforce compliance protocols, and enhance coordination
among regulatory agencies, law enforcement, and the pawnshop industry;
NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, AS IT IS HEREBY RESOLVED, that the
House of Representatives direct the appropriate committee to conduct a
comprehensive review of PD No. 114 and to undertake an inquiry, in aid of legislation,
on its enforcement, with particular focus on:
1. The adequacy of existing rules and procedures for verifying ownership and
documentation of pledged items to prevent the acceptance of stolen property and
the laundering of proceeds of crime;
2. The effectiveness of supervision and enforcement by agencies such as the
Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas, the Anti-Money Laundering Council, and the
Philippine National Police, including their capacity to deter illicit activities and
ensure accountability;
3. The protocols and mechanisms for inter-agency information sharing on
suspicious transactions, stolen property, and persons engaged in money
laundering or fencing;
4. The gaps, challenges, and deficiencies in the implementation of PD No. 114 and
related issuances, and the measures needed to strengthen compliance, training,
monitoring, and reporting; and
5. The sufficiency of penalties, sanctions, and remedies to deter and address
violations, and the possible reforms needed to reinforce deterrence and public
confidence in regulatory enforcement.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED, that the committee shall conduct public hearings, solicit
inputs from stakeholders, including representatives of the pawnshop sector, the
banking community, law-enforcement agencies, civil-society organizations, and
affected individuals, and submit a report containing its findings, conclusions, and
recommendations for appropriate legislative action.
Adopted,
Rep. ROBERT NAZAL
Bagong Henerasyon Party-List

-- 2 of 2 --

ACCESSIBILITY AND RELIABILITY OF WATER SERVICES IN THE CITY
OF...
fullText:
Republic of the Philippines
HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES
Quezon City
TWENTIETH CONGRESS
Third Regular Session
HOUSE RESOLUTION NO. _________
Introduced by REPRESENTATIVE JONATHAN KEITH T. FLORES
RESOLUTION
URGING THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES, THROUGH THE
COMMITTEE ON GOVERNMENT ENTERPRISES AND PRIVATIZATION, TO
CONDUCT AN INVESTIGATION, IN AID OF LEGISLATION, ON THE JOINT
VENTURE AGREEMENT BETWEEN MALAYBALAY CITY WATER
DISTRICT (MCWD) AND PRIMEWATER INFRASTRUCTURE
CORPORATION (PRIMEWATER) OPERATIONS, AND ITS EFFECT ON THE
ACCESSIBILITY AND RELIABILITY OF WATER SERVICES IN THE CITY
OF MALAYBALAY
WHEREAS, access to clean, reliable, and affordable water is a basic human right and a
vital component of public health, economic development and environmental sustainability;
WHEREAS, the Malaybalay City Water District was established pursuant to Presidential
Decree 198, also known as the Provincial Water Utilities Act of 1973, and Resolution No.
3-26-75 passed by the Malaybalay Municipal Council, with the goal of supplying safe and
affordable water to the people of Malaybalay1;
WHEREAS, PrimeWater Infrastructure Corp. entered into a joint venture agreement
(JVA) with the Malaybalay City Water District (MCWD) in 2020 to operate, manage, and
improve water supply services in the city2;
WHEREAS, the residents of Malaybalay City have expressed growing concerns over
deteriorating water service conditions under PrimeWater’s management, including
frequent supply interruptions, low water pressure, and exorbitant fees, among others3;
1https://www.emcwd.gov.ph/history-of-mcwd/
2https://www.rappler.com/philippines/mindanao/coa-urges-malaybalay-city-water-district-take-back-control-primewater-
infrastructure-corporation/
3https://www.rappler.com/philippines/mindanao/malaybalay-water-district-deal-villar-firm-primewater-crisis/
July 3, 2025
3:31 pm
23

-- 1 of 2 --

WHEREAS, these issues have caused widespread inconvenience and hardship to
consumers, and may potentially pose risks to public health and safety - raising serious
concerns on whether PrimeWater has fulfilled its obligations under the joint venture
agreement;
WHEREAS, it is the duty of the House of Representative to ensure transparency,
accountability, and the protection of public interest in all public-private partnerships,
particularly those affecting essential services like water supply;
RESOLVED, that the House of Representatives, through the Committee on Government
Enterprise and Privatization, to conduct an investigation, in aid of legislation, on the joint
venture agreement between Malaybalay City Water District (MCWD) and PrimeWater
Infrastructure Corporation (PRIMEWATER), and its effect on accessibility and reliability
of water services in the City of Malaybalay.
Adopted,
JONATHAN KEITH T. FLORES

-- 2 of 2 --
